titleStmt.title: Letter from Edward O'Farrell to Sir Edward Troup, 27 June 1916
Letters 1916-1923
titleStmt.author: Edward O'Farrell

editionStmt.p: This work was originally published by Maynooth University in Ireland in 2017. In 2026 this data, stored in a relational database was extracted and converted into this TEI/XML document.

availability.p: This is an open access work licensed under Creative Commons Attribution NonCommercial 4.0 International license (CC BY-NC 4.0).

note.p: Edward O'Farrell served as the assistant undersecretary of Ireland after the Easter Rising. Sir Edward Troup (1857-1941) served as permanent Under Secretary of State from 1908 - 1922.

correspAction.placeName: Home Office, Whitehall, London, S.W., England

projectDesc.p: The data in these XML files was generated based on a data dump from the Magellan database (https://github.com/Maynooth-Center-for-Digital-Humanities/Magellan). Each database record has been converted to a JSON file (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/json). The element section of the JSON file contains the TEI-encoded contents of the letters. The TEI XML has been cleaned and made well-formed using a Python script (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/src).

body.ab:
27th June 1916. 10671. Sir, With reference to your letter of the 22nd
instant (311980/10) and enclosures, regarding the
classification of the persons convicted and
sentenced to penal servitude in connection with
the recent Sinn Fein rebellion, I am directed by
the Lords Justices to acquaint you, for the in-
formation of the Secretary of State, that Their
Excellencies are advised that all persons who
were originally either sentenced to death or to
a term of penal servitude come within the category
of Felons notwithstanding any subsequent
commutation of the sentence. The committal warrants which accompanied
your letter are herewith returned. I am,
Sir,
Your obedient Servant,
E O'Farrell The Under Secretary of State,
Home Office,
Whitehall,
London, S.W.
